features.name: Canvas game engine with tile map
features.description: HTML5 Canvas rendering engine with a tile-based map system, greenish background, and pixelated retro aesthetic
features.detailedDescription: Set up the core game engine using HTML5 Canvas. Render a top-down tile map with a greenish color palette. Support a grid-based world that objects and the player character exist within. Use pixel-art style rendering (nearest-neighbor scaling, no anti-aliasing).

acceptanceCriteria.given: The page is loaded in a browser
acceptanceCriteria.then: A top-down tile map is rendered with greenish tiles filling the viewport
All graphics use nearest-neighbor scaling with no anti-aliasing blur

features.name: Arrow-key character movement
features.description: A player character sprite that moves around the tile map using arrow keys, with collision boundaries
features.detailedDescription: Create a character sprite rendered on the canvas. Listen for arrow key input and move the character smoothly across the tile grid. Implement basic collision detection so the character cannot walk off the map. Support sprite animation for walking directions.

acceptanceCriteria.given: The game is loaded and the character is on screen
acceptanceCriteria.when: The player presses an arrow key
acceptanceCriteria.then: The character moves one tile in that direction with a walking animation

acceptanceCriteria.given: The character is at the edge of the map
acceptanceCriteria.when: The player presses an arrow key toward the edge
acceptanceCriteria.then: The character does not move off the map

acceptanceCriteria.given: The player holds an arrow key
acceptanceCriteria.then: The character moves continuously at a consistent pace

features.name: Welcome to the Realm title screen
features.description: A retro-styled title/landing screen with 'Welcome to the Realm' text and a press-start prompt
features.detailedDescription: Display a title screen before gameplay begins. Show 'Welcome to the Realm' in a pixel font with a retro 90s aesthetic. Include a flashing 'Press any key to start' prompt. Transition into the game world when the player presses a key.

acceptanceCriteria.then: 'Welcome to the Realm' is displayed in a pixel font with a retro aesthetic
The title screen transitions to the game world

features.name: Interactive world objects
features.description: Placeable objects on the map that the player can walk up to and interact with via a button press
features.detailedDescription: Define an object system where items can be placed on specific tiles. When the player character is adjacent to an object and presses an action key (spacebar/enter), trigger an interaction. Objects should have pixel-art sprites and visual indicators that they are interactable.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Objects are visible on the map
acceptanceCriteria.when: The player looks at the map
acceptanceCriteria.then: Interactive objects are visible as distinct pixel-art sprites on specific tiles

acceptanceCriteria.given: The character is adjacent to an interactive object
acceptanceCriteria.when: The player presses spacebar or enter
acceptanceCriteria.then: An interaction is triggered (e.g., dialogue box, animation, sound)

acceptanceCriteria.when: The player tries to walk onto that tile
acceptanceCriteria.then: The character is blocked and cannot pass through the object

features.name: Fantasy + sci-fi themed world design
features.description: Themed tile art, objects, and environmental details blending fantasy and sci-fi elements
features.detailedDescription: Design the tile map world with a mix of fantasy and sci-fi theming. Include things like medieval castles next to crashed spaceships, magic crystals beside computer terminals, swords in stones near lightsaber-esque items. The juxtaposition should feel intentional and funny.

acceptanceCriteria.then: Both fantasy elements (trees, castles, crystals) and sci-fi elements (terminals, ships, tech) are present

acceptanceCriteria.when: The player views any area of the map
acceptanceCriteria.then: All art uses a consistent pixel-art style despite the genre mashup

features.name: Absurdist dialogue and interaction system
features.description: A dialogue/text box system for NPC and object interactions featuring absurdist comedy writing
features.detailedDescription: Implement a retro RPG-style text box that appears when interacting with objects or NPCs. Fill it with absurdist comedy — references to LOTR, Star Wars, Star Trek, Mythic Quest, Silicon Valley, Dead Pixels. Dialogue should be unexpected, meta, and funny. Examples: a sword that monologues about its LinkedIn profile, a wizard who only speaks in startup jargon.

acceptanceCriteria.given: The player interacts with an object or NPC
acceptanceCriteria.then: A retro-styled text box appears with dialogue text that types out letter by letter
The content is absurdist comedy with pop culture references

acceptanceCriteria.when: The player presses spacebar or enter
acceptanceCriteria.then: The dialogue box closes and the player regains control

features.name: Themed NPCs and interactive content
features.description: Populate the world with funny NPCs and interactable items that reference favorite shows and absurdist humor
features.detailedDescription: Create a cast of NPCs and objects that bring the world to life. Ideas: a Gandalf-like wizard who is also a sysadmin, a stormtrooper who missed his calling as a bard, a quest giver who just wants you to fix the Wi-Fi. Each interaction should be a self-contained comedic bit. Objects could include: a mysterious orb that shows you a 'terms of service' scroll, a treasure chest that contains only a single sock.

acceptanceCriteria.then: At least 5 distinct NPCs are placed around the world with unique sprites

acceptanceCriteria.given: The player interacts with different NPCs/objects
acceptanceCriteria.then: Each NPC/object has its own unique dialogue or interaction behavior

features.name: 90s web nostalgia polish
features.description: Visual flair inspired by 90s websites: hit counter, under construction GIF, guestbook, visitor counter, marquee text
features.detailedDescription: Add 90s web nostalgia elements as either in-game objects or UI chrome around the canvas. Ideas: a fake hit counter at the bottom ('You are visitor #000,042'), a spinning 'under construction' GIF somewhere on the page, a marquee-scrolling message, comic sans in strategic places, a fake guestbook link. These elements should be both nostalgic and played for comedy.

acceptanceCriteria.when: The visitor views the full page
acceptanceCriteria.then: At least 3 classic 90s web elements are visible (hit counter, under construction, marquee, etc.)
The elements have a humorous twist (e.g., hit counter stuck at 42, guestbook signed by fictional characters)
